title: Grayscale Files S-1 for Spot Worldcoin ETF
dek: Grayscale filed an S-1 registration statement with the SEC on July 20, 2026, to launch a spot Worldcoin (WLD) ETF under file number 333-297570. The filing is an early step in the approval process and does not guarantee the fund will trade; SEC approval and exchange rule clearance are both required.
author: Crypto Cronkite
key_fact: Grayscale submitted an SEC S-1 registration for a spot Worldcoin ETF, signaling institutional appetite for single-asset altcoin equity products beyond Bitcoin and Ethereum.
bottom_line: Watch for the SEC's first public comment or determination on the S-1, which will signal whether regulators view altcoin ETFs as viable equity products. A material development would be any SEC request for additional information, or renewed scrutiny of Worldcoin's regulatory status in major jurisdictions, either of which could delay or condition approval.
body: Grayscale filed an S-1 registration statement with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on July 20, 2026, to launch a spot Worldcoin (WLD) ETF, according to The Defiant. The filer entity is Grayscale Worldcoin ETF, registered under file number 333-297570 and accession number 0001193125-26-308957. The filing fee exhibit lists the offering as "Grayscale Worldcoin ETF Shares," classified as Exchange-Traded Vehicle Securities.

The product would hold WLD, the native token of the Worldcoin project, which operates the World Network identity system built around iris-scanning "orb" devices. At the time of reporting, WLD traded up 4.5% over 24 hours to a circulating market capitalization of approximately $1.35 billion, per The Defiant. Bitcoin was up 2.6% over the same window.

The S-1 is an early procedural step in the ETF approval process and does not guarantee the fund will begin trading. The registration statement must become effective and the listing exchange must clear its own rule-change process before shares can be offered. Grayscale has not published a fee, ticker symbol, or listing venue for the Worldcoin product in the initial S-1 filing.

Grayscale has previously filed for ETF products covering other alternative assets such as Solana and Zcash. The Stamford, Connecticut-based firm has built a track record of single-asset trust and ETF products serving institutional and retail exposure to crypto assets.

The bear case is regulatory and procedural. The S-1 filing does not guarantee approval; sources do not discuss any regulatory concerns, criticisms, or prior rejections of Grayscale altcoin ETF filings. No timeline for SEC review or approval decision is provided. More structurally, the reporting does not address Worldcoin-specific regulatory risks such as the privacy and identity concerns around the iris-scanning orb technology that regulators or the public might raise. The sources also do not specify what timeline the SEC is working to or whether other Grayscale altcoin ETF applications have faced delays or conditions.
